mnemonics.title: UTP Categories
mnemonics.content: Cat 3 (Voice), Cat 5 (100M), Cat 5e (1G), Cat 6 (10G) - 3-5-5e-6: Think "35e6" for data rates

acronyms.usage: Measure of signal quality; higher is better
Used in cable TV and DSL

visualTips.visualization: Think of fiber optic like a 'light pipe' - core (center) surrounded by cladding (reflective coating), all protected by jacket. Light bounces inside due to total internal reflection.
Imagine two wires spiraling like DNA - this twisting cancels out EMI and crosstalk.
Layer cake: Copper core (center) → Insulation → Braided shield → Outer jacket. Like a bullet: tip, plastic, metal casing, cover.
Single-mode: one straight highway lane (single light path). Multi-mode: many wide lanes (multiple light paths), causing dispersion.

analogies.analogy: Like roads: guided media are 'roads with lanes' (cables), unguided are 'air traffic' (wireless). Just as trucks vs planes have different capacities and uses.
Like a flashlight's beam getting dimmer over distance - signal weakens the farther it travels.
Like pipe width - wider pipes (higher bandwidth) can carry more water (data) simultaneously.
Like people talking simultaneously in a quiet room - adjacent conversations interfere with each other.
